incidents.vector: ShinyHunters-linked actors stole authentication tokens from analytics vendor Anodot via voice and email phishing, then used the tokens to access cloud environments of more than a dozen Anodot customers without breaching them directly.
incidents.impact: Vimeo confirmed unauthorized access to user and customer data including video titles, metadata, and customer email addresses; the same Anodot supply-chain compromise has been tied by the attackers to recent breaches at Rockstar Games, McGraw Hill, and ADT. Vimeo disabled Anodot credentials and notified law enforcement. No video content, logins, or payment data accessed.

incidents.industry: Multi-sector (universities, law firms, defense contractors, healthcare research, think tanks)
incidents.attacker: China — Ministry of State Security (MSS) contractor
incidents.vector: Chinese state contractor exploited Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day vulnerabilities to steal COVID-19 vaccine research and US policy data; the accused operator was extradited from Italy to face US charges.
incidents.impact: DOJ accuses Xu Zewei, working under China's Shanghai State Security Bureau, of helping compromise more than 12,700 US organizations during the pandemic; he faces up to 62 years in prison after his first US court appearance in Houston.

incidents.victim: Retail and hospitality companies (multi-sector since February)
incidents.vector: Voice phishing actors impersonate IT support to steal credentials, then pivot into senior accounts via further social engineering to access SaaS, Microsoft Graph, Salesforce, SharePoint and internal repositories; some operators have swatted executives to pressure ransom payments.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 and RH-ISAC report opportunistic seven-figure extortion demands from 'BlackFile/Cordial Spider' against retail and hospitality victims since February; group runs a data-leak site and overlaps with the broader 'Com'-linked voice-phishing wave.

incidents.vector: Attackers flood targeted inboxes with spam, then impersonate IT support over Microsoft Teams to lure victims into installing a malicious browser extension and follow-on malware.
incidents.impact: Mandiant reports 'UNC6692' campaign deploys SnowBelt browser extension as a backdoor giving persistent access to corporate accounts and internal systems; campaign uses an Edge-only login overlay and intentionally rejects the first password attempts to harvest credentials twice.

incidents.vector: Spear-phishing campaign targeting top German decision makers via the Signal messaging app to steal session data.
incidents.impact: German officials suspect Russia is behind the campaign; affected individuals were notified, and authorities say the data leak has been stopped but cannot rule out additional victims.

incidents.victim: Navigate360 (P3 Global Intel school tip line)
incidents.vector: Attackers exploited platform vulnerabilities in the P3 Global Intel anonymous tip line used by 30,000+ schools and 5,000 public safety agencies, then released stolen data on a public leak site.
incidents.impact: Hackers claim to have stolen 93 GB including students' personally identifiable information, raising concerns the 'anonymous' tip line is no longer trusted; Senators Hassan and Banks demanded answers about Navigate360's safeguards. Attacker messaging frames the leak as anti-policing/hacktivist rather than financial.

incidents.victim: npm / GitHub / Docker / VS Code Marketplace ecosystem (LiteLLM, Checkmarx, Trivy, Telnyx, Axios, Bitwarden CLI)
incidents.vector: Group calling itself TeamPCP used stolen developer credentials to push trojanized packages to widely used registries; payloads steal cloud, npm and GitHub tokens to backdoor downstream packages and CI/CD pipelines, with self-replicating 'Shai-Hulud' worm behavior.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 and Recorded Future detail compromised LiteLLM (~97M monthly downloads), Checkmarx Docker/VS Code/GitHub Action artifacts, Trivy, Telnyx PyPI, Axios npm, and a fake @bitwarden/cli npm package; group claims 300 GB+ of stolen data and is reportedly working with extortion partners. Downstream impact across thousands of enterprise CI/CD environments still unfolding.

incidents.victim: US federal agencies and global organizations running Cisco ASA / Firepower
incidents.vector: APT actor exploits CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA firmware, then deploys the FIRESTARTER backdoor inside the LINA process and manipulates the Cisco Service Platform mount list to persist across firmware patches.
incidents.impact: CISA, NCSC-UK, Talos and ACSC issued joint warnings and updated Emergency Directive ED 25-03 after detecting FIRESTARTER on patched federal Cisco devices; defenders must collect forensic data, apply new vendor updates, and assume compromise even on previously patched units. Same actor cluster previously linked to ArcaneDoor espionage on perimeter network gear.

incidents.victim: US victims of Cambodian scam-center fraud
incidents.vector: Pig-butchering and romance-scam centers in Cambodia recruited (and trafficked) operators who lured US victims into cryptocurrency investment fraud, laundering proceeds through casino and office-park front operations.
incidents.impact: OFAC sanctioned Cambodian Senator Kok An and 28 individuals/entities in his network; the Scam Center Strike Force seized 503 fraudulent crypto-investment domains and a recruitment messaging app, and State offered a $10M reward for info on the Tai Chang compound. USSS previously tied $73M+ in laundered victim funds to the network.

incidents.victim: Axios npm package and downstream consumers
incidents.vector: DPRK-linked operators (tracked as STARDUST CHOLLIMA) compromised the popular Axios npm package and prioritized theft of npm tokens and GitHub Personal Access Tokens to extend access into CI/CD pipelines.
incidents.impact: CrowdStrike attributes the Axios npm compromise to a North Korean cluster moving toward systematic supply-chain access for revenue generation; impact details are still being assessed but the package is broadly used in JavaScript applications.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion forced the city to take its IT network offline; surrounding Leon County severed connections to prevent contamination — typical ransomware containment behavior.
incidents.impact: Multiple Tallahassee city websites and public services were unavailable for days; city leadership has not publicly confirmed ransomware but response posture is consistent with one.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and crypto investors (2022 incidents)
incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — SIM-swap and SMS phishing
incidents.vector: Tens of thousands of SMS phishing messages tricked tech-company employees into surrendering credentials, which the gang used to access internal systems and execute SIM-swap attacks on cryptocurrency holders.
incidents.impact: Tyler Robert Buchanan ('Tylerb'), a senior Scattered Spider member, pleaded guilty in US federal court to wire fraud and aggravated identity theft tied to the 2022 hacks of Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp and others, and to stealing at least $8M in crypto. He faces up to 22 years; sentencing August 2026.

incidents.vector: OT-targeted malware ('ZionSiphon') hardcoded to scan Israeli IP ranges and look for water-treatment and desalination management software, then push pump pressure and chlorine levels to maximum.
incidents.impact: Darktrace researchers found the malware never triggered in the wild because of an XOR encoding bug in its IP-range check; the strain self-deletes on non-Israeli networks. Embedded strings reference 'support for our brothers in Iran, Palestine, and Yemen' and 'poisoning the population of Tel Aviv and Haifa,' consistent with prior Iranian targeting of Israeli water OT.

incidents.vector: Initial intrusion traced to malware disguised as Roblox cheats; ShinyHunters-linked actors exfiltrated customer data and offered it for sale.
incidents.impact: Vercel confirmed a breach affecting a small set of customers, told them to rotate keys and secrets, and is now seeing fallout expand to additional customers and third-party systems integrated with Vercel.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors and militaries in Greece, Romania, and Serbia
incidents.vector: Email phishing campaign targeting prosecutors' and military mailboxes attributed to APT28, the GRU's military cyber unit.
incidents.impact: Russian operators broke into the email accounts of more than 170 Ukrainian prosecutors to harvest investigative information, with the same campaign also breaching military networks in Greece, Romania, and Serbia.

incidents.vector: Data stolen from McGraw Hill's Salesforce tenant during the broader voice-phishing-driven Salesforce campaign; ShinyHunters published the data after a failed extortion attempt.
incidents.impact: Personal details of 13.5M users — names, home addresses, emails, and phone numbers — were leaked publicly after McGraw Hill declined to pay ransom; part of the same Salesforce/Anodot intrusion wave hitting multiple US firms.

incidents.victim: Operators of Rockwell Automation / Allen-Bradley OT environments
incidents.vector: Iranian cluster (Cyber Av3ngers / Storm-0784, tracked as CL-STA-1128) shifted from Unitronics PLCs to Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk and Allen-Bradley PLCs, hosting FactoryTalk on attacker VPS infrastructure to support exploitation.
incidents.impact: Unit 42 and CISA observed renewed Iranian targeting of OT/ICS during the ongoing US/Israel-Iran conflict, with Cortex Xpanse seeing 5,600 exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley devices globally; the campaign coincides with Iran restoring partial internet connectivity after a 47-day blackout.

incidents.vector: Arbitrary code execution zero-day (CVE-2026-34621) in Adobe Acrobat and Reader for Windows and macOS exploited via crafted PDFs.
incidents.impact: Adobe issued an emergency out-of-band patch on April 11 after Tenable reported the vulnerability had been exploited in the wild since at least November 2025 — meaning attackers had ~5 months of unpatched access via PDF readers before remediation.

incidents.vector: Pro-Russian hacktivist intrusion attempted to disrupt plant operations; intrusion was caught by built-in safety systems before causing an outage.
incidents.impact: Sweden's minister of civil defense publicly attributed an early-2025 destructive cyberattack attempt against a thermal power plant to actors connected to Russia's intelligence and security services; no operational disruption occurred but confirms continued Russian targeting of European energy infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Attackers exploited a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO platform to mint and withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: $292 million in crypto assets stolen, making it the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date; impact rippling across DeFi restaking ecosystem.

incidents.vector: Active in-the-wild exploitation of CVE-2026-32201, a SharePoint Server spoofing flaw allowing attackers to impersonate trusted content for phishing and social-engineering pivots; April patch also addresses 'BlueHammer' (CVE-2026-33825) Windows Defender privilege escalation with public exploit code.
incidents.impact: Microsoft's record-setting April Patch Tuesday addressed 167 vulnerabilities including the actively exploited SharePoint zero-day, eight critical bugs (Windows IKE RCE, .NET DoS, Office RCE chains, AD RCE, TCP/IP RCE) and a SQL Server remote code execution vulnerability. Defenders should prioritize the SharePoint patch given confirmed exploitation.
